The Indian government aggressively utilized the weekend to clear the massive, crippling backlog of cargo at its major national ports following the recent, massive nationwide dock workers' strike. ## SECTION 7 — This Day in History (World)
On January 29, 1886, Karl Benz, a massive, highly brilliant German mechanical engineer, officially patented the "Motorwagen." The massive, highly innovative three-wheeled vehicle was powered by an internal combustion engine and is universally recognized as the world's very first practical, commercially available automobile. This monumental patent fundamentally initiated the massive global automotive industry, completely revolutionizing human transportation, urban design, and the massive, geopolitical reliance on fossil fuels.

On January 29, 1845, Edgar Allan Poe's massive, deeply atmospheric, and hauntingly rhythmic poem "The Raven" was officially published for the first time in the *New York Evening Mirror*. The poem became a massive, instantaneous critical and popular sensation, fundamentally cementing Poe's massive literary reputation. Its exploration of grief, madness, and the supernatural heavily influenced the massive development of the global Gothic and symbolist literary movements.

## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (India)
On January 29, 1780, the massive, highly historic *Hicky's Bengal Gazette* (also known as the *Original Calcutta General Advertiser*) was published by an eccentric Irishman named James Augustus Hicky. It was the absolute, very first printed newspaper published anywhere on the Indian subcontinent. Despite its short, massive two-year lifespan due to British censorship, it fundamentally established the massive, highly powerful tradition of independent print journalism in India.

On January 29, 1939, the massive, highly respected Ramakrishna Mission Institute of Culture was officially founded in Calcutta (Kolkata). Established to commemorate the massive birth centenary of Sri Ramakrishna, the institute became a massive, premier center for the advanced study and global dissemination of traditional Indian philosophy, literature, and arts, heavily promoting massive, intercultural dialogue and the universal, philosophical principles of Vedanta.
